calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Carl Salceido, #2031.

Joshua A. Reiten, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Sunset Scavenger Company and Golden Gate Disposal & Recycling Company.
No appearance by defendants Aroldo Perez, a/k/a Aroldo Mejia, Pablo Mejia Perez, a/k/a Pablo Mejia and Pablo Perez, Pablo Mejia Gonzalez, a/k/a Pablo Timoteo Mejia, Noe Eber Mejia Lopez, Rolando Gomez, Carlos Lopez, Joel Gonzalez, Catalino Bamaca Mejia, Valerio Hernandez, Angel Coronado or Henry Saul Yoc Bamaca, a/k/a Henry Yaul Yoc.
Defaults were entered against defendants on March 26, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on October 7, 2009.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed December 9, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Sunset Scavenger Company and Golden Gate Disposal & Recycling Company, and against the Defaulted Defendants Aroldo Perez, a/k/a Aroldo Mejia, Pablo Mejia Perez, a/k/a Pablo Mejia and Pablo Perez, Pablo Mejia Gonzalez, a/k/a Pablo Timoteo Mejia, Noe Eber Mejia Lopez, Rolando Gomez, Carlos Lopez, Joel Gonzalez, Catalino Bamaca Mejia, Valerio Hernandez, Angel Coronado or Henry Saul Yoc Bamaca, a/k/a Henry Yaul Yoc, as follows:

Defendants are permanently enjoined from taking, removing or otherwise appropriating any container bearing the words "Norcal Waste Systems, Inc.," "Sunset Scavenger Co.," or "Golden Gate Recycling & Disposal Co." that is located on a public street or sidewalk for the collection of recyclable material from a residence or business; and/or any material in a container bearing the words "Norcal Waste Systems, Inc.," "Sunset Scavenger Co.," or "Golden Gate Recycling & Disposal Co." that is located in a placed designated by a business for the collection of recyclable material.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause re: preliminary Injunction, pursuant to ex parte Order filed on January 15, 2009. Appearances: Joshua A. Reiten, Esq. of Howard Rice Nemerovski Canady Falk & Rabkin, (415) 434-1600, appearing for Plaintiffs Sunset Scavenger Company, and Golden Gate Disposal & Recycling Company; There are no other appearances. Counsel appearing to have Order signed. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. No opposition filed. The Court also, adds to its ruling that Plaintiffs shall file with the Clerk of the Court by February 27, 2009, an undertaking in the amount of $1,000 pending trial of this action. Order Granting Preliminary Injunction signed in Open Court. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
